flasher.atomizerBoundary: Atomizer retains operational firmware compatibility only and exposes no firmware-update operation.

assumptions: Atomizer launches a separately built SignalLab bridge whose first stdout line exactly satisfies measurement contract version 1

the executable and public contract are non-writable regular non-symbolic-link files owned by the application user or root and resolved from an explicit override, packaged resource, or declared sibling development repository
ready identity binds the exact contract, catalog, and shipped generator JavaScript hashes
one admitted session permits one in-flight request and treats framing, process, correlation, schema, or timeout failure as terminal
guarantees: SignalLab produces only high-level swept-spectrum and detected-power measurements qualified synthetic-visual-projection
SignalLab claims usbEmulated false, firmwareExecuted false, and rfEmitted false and exposes no USB, firmware, serial, screen, touch, RF-generator, or complex-IQ identity
every status and measurement carries the exact ready identity while Atomizer binds the session sourceId independently
selected profile is visible only in status and capability state and is never copied into measurement or classifier evidence
profile selection invalidates the admitted acquisition configuration before dispatch, atomically changes the selected capability, and must advance the opaque producer configuration epoch

every SignalLab session, profile result, and measurement is bound to the exact current opaque producer configuration epoch; stale, missing, unchanged-after-mutation, or mismatched epochs fault the session
requests and lines are bounded, execution is serial, every accepted request settles once, and neither party retries or falls back

assumptions: serial enumeration retains valid endpoints and typed source-scoped failures independently
the selected candidate identity is exact-zs407-cdc at USB VID:PID 0483:5740
firmware shell identifies ZS407 across the version and info responses
reported source revision resolves through Atomizer's operational compatibility registry or is explicitly warning-admitted as custom-unqualified
firmware exposes the complete required command set including zero offset readback
guarantees: execution is physical and qualification is device-observed
usbIdentityVerified is true only after exact identity
raw serial and TinySA shell semantics remain below the tinysa-zs407 driver
serial enumeration, open, and close are bounded; a late open is closed as an orphan, an uncertain close blocks writes, and unrelated serial endpoints are never admitted

RF output state is command-acknowledged rather than measured; dispatch uncertainty, acknowledgement failure, or state-echo disagreement becomes unknown and faults the session until explicit output-off recovery
scanraw offset is read immediately before binary acquisition and retained as evidence
the generic host connects a physical candidate only after explicit selection or a persisted tinysa-zs407 preference

assumptions: the tinysa-zs407 driver descriptor-launches the admitted owner-controlled, non-writable declared sibling bridge with contractVersion 1 under an environment allowlist
ready evidence exactly matches the pinned executable-twin release, source commit, binary hash, bridge, and boot signature
guarantees: execution and qualification are firmware-executed-twin
usbIdentityVerified is absent and usbTransactionsModeled is false
RF output state is firmware-executed-twin evidence from the twin and never a claim of physical emission or calibrated RF measurement
sweeps, LCD frames, touch, and generator state originate from executable firmware or fail
the generic host connects the twin only after explicit selection or a persisted tinysa-zs407/tinysa-firmware-twin preference

assumptions: a future Firmware-owned sink explicitly accepts SignalLabStimulusIntent contractVersion 1
guarantees: the active SignalLab-to-Atomizer measurement edge does not activate or imply a Firmware stimulus sink
SignalLab never impersonates USB hardware
standards-derived profiles remain visual resource and timing projections unless immutable conformance evidence is added
no current process applies SignalLab stimulus intent to executable firmware

admission.factoryDefault: load the owner-only version-1 preference or select the signal-lab driver when no preference file exists
admission.discovery: complete one generation across the static driver registry while retaining valid candidates and typed driver/source-scoped failures independently
admission.selection: select exactly one candidate owned by the preferred driver and optional source kind
admission.ambiguity: fail visibly when a preference matches zero or multiple candidates
admission.fallback: never connect a different driver or source kind after preference, discovery, admission, bridge, identity, or connection failure
admission.operatorSwitch: manual selection may connect SignalLab, a physical ZS407, or the executable firmware twin after the current session is RF-safely disconnected

safetyInvariants: No component silently retries, reroutes, substitutes, downgrades, or fabricates successful evidence.
SignalLab simulation, physical USB, Renode firmware execution, and future SDR acquisition retain distinct sourceKind, execution, transport, qualification, capability, and provenance variants.
Every session candidate, provenance record, configuration revision, measurement, and feature result is runtime-validated at driver, manager, IPC, and renderer boundaries.
The factory default is SignalLab; preference or startup failure is visible and cannot activate a TinySA or firmware twin as fallback.
Only Atomizer owns runtime instrument selection, session serialization, configuration admission, acquisition streaming, OpenAI credentials, agent policy, approvals, and operator orchestration.

Each driver owns source-specific discovery, identity proof, command translation, capabilities, and teardown; the generic host contains no serial, TinySA shell, SignalLab generator, or future NeptuneSDR protocol logic.
SignalLab is a high-level measurement driver and is never represented as a ByteTransport, TinySA, USB device, firmware device, RF emitter, screen, touch surface, or complex-IQ source.
SignalLab selected profile and UI state are excluded from detector and classifier inputs; only admitted measurement evidence and source qualification may reach analysis.
A SignalLab profile change invalidates the current configuration before any further acquisition.

A sweep or detected-power capture is admitted only when its session ID, configuration revision, provenance binding, point count, monotonic sequence, units, and capability constraints match the active session.

One acquisition is one atomic measurement transaction: a driver's event and return must be deeply equal when both exist, exactly one validated result is projected, and a conflicting, repeated, novel, stale, or out-of-band measurement faults the session.

Complex-IQ v1 is a bounded single-buffer acquisition of at most 64 MiB reserved for a driver such as NeptuneSDR: acquire returns one complete cf32le Uint8Array with exact sample-count geometry; continuous complex-IQ acquisition is rejected, and v1 claims no chunking, continuation, backpressure, or indefinite stream semantics. Current SignalLab and TinySA drivers do not claim it.

Atomizer main is the sole owner of RF output state. Physical state is command-acknowledged, executable-twin state is firmware-executed-twin, and unsupported sources are not-applicable; none of those labels implies calibrated RF measurement.

An RF-capable session enters unknown before output-affecting dispatch. Configuration and each acquisition establish output off, disconnect reasserts output off, and command failure, incomplete echo, or uncertain touch fails closed without claiming off.

Firmware-screen touch invalidates the admitted acquisition configuration and RF state; acquisition and unsafe features remain blocked until an explicit output-off recovery establishes a safe session state.

Serial discovery, open, and close have finite deadlines; only exact 0483:5740 endpoints may reach physical ZS407 admission, late-open handles are closed, uncertain-close transports reject writes, and unrelated serial bytes cannot enter a session.
Every main-to-renderer response and event is runtime-validated before it may alter renderer state; stale-session events and stale producer epochs are rejected.
Only Firmware owns executable-twin state and the future SignalLab stimulus sink.
Only SignalLab owns waveform descriptors, deterministic channel configuration, synthetic measurement generation, and stimulus intent.

TinySA_Flasher is outside the runtime trio and exclusively owns OEM and custom firmware artifacts, download or import verification, physical preflight, DFU admission, firmware writes, durable update journals, and post-write verification.
Atomizer exposes no firmware artifact, update, DFU, journal, or flash API and does not import or launch TinySA_Flasher.
The runtime trio and TinySA_Flasher have no circular source, build, package, process, or state dependency.
RF output enable and general firmware-screen touch remain action-time approval boundaries for Atom.
Every Atom UI hook resolves to exactly one typed tool contract, risk class, evidence projection, executor, and guarantee.
Screenshots, device strings, application context, bridge diagnostics, and tool outputs are untrusted data rather than instructions.
Persistent Realtime sessions expose only load_atom_tools; one loader response selects one to eight exact registered schemas, and application calls outside that response-scoped set are rejected.
Atomizer configures no output-token cap or reduced truncation window; throughput is controlled through static instructions, pull-based state, and response-scoped schemas.
The exact AI model is gpt-realtime-2.1 on Realtime API paths; model, endpoint, or transport substitution is forbidden.
liveness: Every admitted runtime request settles exactly once as success or explicit failure.
Continuous acquisition terminates on explicit stop, disconnect, configuration invalidation, profile change, renderer loss, shutdown, or first operation failure.
SignalLab boot, twin boot, and every bridge request are bounded by declared timeouts.
A poisoned or stopped firmware-twin bridge is reaped as a complete isolated process group; no descendant may survive teardown.
A SignalLab bridge shutdown acknowledgement is followed by bounded process exit; unexpected stdout, stderr framing, process exit, or timeout poisons the session without retry.
Instrument shutdown commands RF output off when that capability exists before disconnecting the active session; failed shutdown remains retryable without discarding the owned session.
Physical serial enumeration, open, and close settle within declared deadlines, including cleanup of handles returned after an open timeout.
Realtime session configuration must be acknowledged and recursively match every sent setting before operation proceeds.
TinySA_Flasher availability or update state cannot block Atomizer, Firmware, or SignalLab startup or operation.

compatibility.rule: A producer guarantee must imply every consumer assumption on the same active runtime edge.
compatibility.runtimeVersioning: Any weakened guarantee, stronger assumption, changed runtime owner, changed evidence label, driver contract change, or newly active runtime edge requires a new trio contract version and coordinated commits in all three runtime repositories.
compatibility.driverEvolution: A new driver registers a unique driverId and sourceKind set, declares only truthful generic capabilities, and adds a separately versioned source-specific extension only when the common contract cannot express an operation without making another driver lie; complex-IQ chunking, continuation, backpressure, or long-lived sample streaming requires a new instrument/API contract version.
compatibility.externalUtilityVersioning: TinySA_Flasher versions its application, device, evidence, and interface contracts independently; changing its release or write policy does not change this runtime composition unless the ownership boundary changes.
compatibility.verification: Release gating requires byte-identical v4 copies in the three runtime repositories, a real SignalLab producer-consumer interoperation test, repository-local runtime/build/boundary tests, and independent validation of TinySA_Flasher's active interface catalog v3, active application/device contract v2, frozen interface catalog v2, and frozen legacy application contract v1.
